steps.title: Identify the causal claim
steps.content: The student concludes that variations in the IGF-1 gene “must account for” the size differences—implying this single gene is the sole explanation.

steps.title: Find what undermines the conclusion
steps.content:
Choice A directly contradicts this by identifying the IGF-1 gene as “one of multiple genes” that affect size; if other genes also contribute, one gene alone cannot account for all the variance.

B shifts to generalizability across species (irrelevant to dogs), C affirms the gene's importance (supporting rather than undermining), and D discusses variant prevalence rather than explanatory power.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
